writ.title: Continuing: Periodic tick for the Reckoner
writ.body:
# Continuing: Periodic tick for the Reckoner

## Situation

You are continuing implementation work begun in a prior session. The
prior session delivered the Reckoner core source migration to the new
periodic-tick model and committed it as a single checkpoint
(`feat(reckoner): periodic tick replaces the CDC handler â€” partial`).

The reckoner package builds clean at this checkpoint and its primary
test surface (`reckoner-tick.test.ts`, 13 tests) passes. **Cascade
tests in adjacent files and the documentation are not yet updated**
â€” they still reflect the pre-tick CDC model and are red against the
new code.

Your job is to complete the cascades.

## What the prior session committed

- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/types.ts` â€” `ReckoningDeferReason`
  extended with `dependency_pending` and `dependency_failed`.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/tick.ts` â€” **new file**. The
  `runTick()` handler, the `reckoner.tick` relay contribution, and
  the `@every 60s` standing-order contribution. ~700 lines.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner.ts` â€” refactored. CDC
  observer (`handleWritsChange`), per-writ-update path (`considerWrit`
  entry, `runScheduler` per-call shape), and `runCatchUpScan` startup
  pass all removed. The apparatus now declares
  `recommends: ['clockworks']` and exposes `runTick(event?)` as the
  test-hook entry point.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-cdc.test.ts` â€” **deleted**
  (945 lines). The CDC test surface is obsolete.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-tick.test.ts` â€” **new
  file** (742 lines). The replacement test surface for the tick model.
  13 tests, all passing.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner.test.ts` â€” small fixture
  tweak to align with the new shape.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml` â€”
  added `@shardworks/clockworks-apparatus` dependency.

## What remains

The following files still reflect the pre-tick CDC model and need to
be updated. They are independent of one another â€” pick any order.

### Reckoner-internal test cascades

- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-scheduler.test.ts` â€”
  `runScheduler` signature changed; test fixtures need to drive
  `runTick(event?)` instead of the per-writ-update path. Reckonings
  row writes are now batched per tick, not per CDC update.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-depends-on.test.ts` â€”
  same: switch from CDC-update-driven harness to tick-driven harness.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/integration.test.ts` â€” end-to-end
  fixture (Stacks + Clerk + Reckoner). Update to drive tick fires
  rather than CDC observer triggers.

### Vision-keeper cascade (collateral)

- `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src/integration.test.ts` â€”
  vision-keeper's tests assumed Reckoner's CDC handler would
  auto-accept their petitions on creation. With CDC gone they need
  to drive a tick fire to get acceptance.
- `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src/vision-keeper.test.ts` â€”
  same dependency on CDC auto-ack.

### Documentation cascade

The four documentation files still describe the CDC model and the
"absence of a row is a defer signal" guidance, both of which the
new tick model contradicts. Reconcile each end-to-end:

- `docs/architecture/apparatus/reckoner.md` â€” primary spec doc.
  Update Â§"What the Reckoner does NOT do (in v0)" (CDC was the v0
  model) and Â§"Outcome mapping" (defer rows are now emitted).
- `docs/architecture/petitioner-registration.md` â€” Â§"Reckoner
  behavior" names the three outcomes; update the Defer bullet for
  the new dependency-aware defers and the per-tick cadence.
- `docs/architecture/reckonings-book.md` â€” defer-reasons enum
  gains `dependency_pending` and `dependency_failed`; the
  "deferUntil and deferSignal" guidance needs a carve-out for
  dependency-defer rows.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/README.md` â€” high-level apparatus
  prose; update for the tick model.

### Verification at completion

- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus test` must pass.
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus test` must pass.
- `grep -rn "CDC handler\|runCatchUpScan\|considerWrit" docs/` must
  return zero hits in the four docs above (any surviving CDC
  references signal incomplete cascade).

---

# Periodic tick for the Reckoner

## Intent

Switch the Reckoner from CDC-driven per-writ-update evaluation to a
periodic tick. Add a `reckoner.tick` relay handler that, on each fire,
queries all currently-held petitions, runs them as a batch through the
configured scheduler, and applies the resulting decisions. Kit-contribute
a standing order with `schedule: '@every 60s'` targeting the relay.
Remove the existing CDC handler on `clerk/writs`, the `runCatchUpScan`
startup pass, and the per-writ-update entry into `considerWrit`.

## Motivation

Today's Reckoner is reactive: a CDC handler on `clerk/writs` runs the
per-action consider-flow on every writ update, plus a startup catch-up
scan picks up pre-existing held petitions. This works for a single-writ
stub like always-approve, but the design intent (per c-mod99ris and
c-moisx6fx) is for schedulers to weigh the whole candidate set against
priorities and capacity â€” a global view the per-writ path can't offer.
The tick gives the scheduler that global view, and unifies pre-existing
held writs with new ones into a single evaluation path.

For the always-approve v0 default, the visible change is a latency
shift: a held petition now waits up to one tick interval (60s) before
approval, instead of being approved on its CDC update. The trade is
acceptable because the next scheduler â€” priority-walk and beyond â€”
depends on the global-view path being the canonical one.

## Non-negotiable decisions

### Schedule â€” hard-coded `@every 60s` in the kit contribution

The Reckoner's kit contribution declares `schedule: '@every 60s'` as
a fixed value. No `reckoner.tickSchedule` config knob in this
commission. Operators wanting different cadence wait until a real need
surfaces (parked future work).

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-5).

### Tick replaces CDC entirely â€” single evaluation path

The CDC handler on `clerk/writs`, the `runCatchUpScan` startup pass,
and any per-writ-update entry into `considerWrit` are removed. The tick
is the only path that drives scheduler evaluation. New petitions wait
up to 60s before first evaluation; pre-existing held petitions get
evaluated on the first tick after apparatus start.

`considerWrit` either becomes the per-writ branch invoked from the
tick loop or its logic folds into the tick handler directly â€”
implementer's call.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-7).

### Tick handler â€” fixed sequence per fire

On each fire of the `reckoner.tick` relay:

1. Resolve the active scheduler from the registry (cached at start by
   the scheduler-registry commission).
2. Re-read `reckoner.schedulerConfig` from `guild.json`. Validate via
   the resolved scheduler's `validateConfig` if present. Throw â†’ log
   fail-loud, skip this tick.
3. Query candidates: held petitions are writs in their initial-equivalent
   phase carrying `ext.reckoner`. Use the same query shape today's
   `runCatchUpScan` uses (find-by-phase, then filter to those with
   `ext.reckoner`).
4. Compose the `SchedulerInput` from the candidate set + petitioner
   metadata + the running tick id.
5. Call `scheduler.evaluate(input)`. The returned decisions are an
   array of `{ writId, outcome, reason? }`.
6. For each decision, in input order: apply the outcome via the same
   transition path the CDC handler used, write a Reckonings row, and
   continue. Per-decision failure is logged and does not abort the
   tick â€” the next decision still runs.

The tick handler runs synchronously from the relay's `run()` callback;
do not introduce async fan-out. A single tick can take seconds; that
is acceptable.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-3, D-7).

### Defer outcomes always emit a Reckonings row

Every consideration outcome â€” accept, defer, decline â€” emits a row.
The pre-tick model treated `defer` as "absence of a row is the signal."
That model is wrong for tick-driven evaluation: without a defer row,
operators can't see why a petition is sitting and cannot diagnose
deferral cycles.

The defer row carries:
- `outcome: 'deferred'`
- `deferReason` from the scheduler's decision reason (`'priority' |
  'queue_depth' | 'time_hold' | 'patron_policy' | 'other'` for
  scheduler-emitted defers; `'dependency_pending' | 'dependency_failed'`
  for dependency-aware defers in a parallel commission).
- `deferNote: <reason string>` (free-form, from the decision).
- `deferCount`, `firstDeferredAt`, `lastDeferredAt`: running counters
  derived from prior deferred rows for the same `writId`.
- `deferUntil` and `deferSignal`: optional, populated when the
  decision carries them (scheduler-driven defers only â€” the
  dependency-aware defer rows in the parallel commission populate
  neither, per the polling-only stance).

Source: c-modaqnpt, c-mod9a54n.

### Pre-seal tick fail-loud

A tick that fires before the scheduler-registry seal (`activeScheduler`
unresolved) MUST fail loud:

```
[reckoner] tick: activeScheduler not resolved â€” scheduler registry
            not yet sealed. Cannot evaluate this tick.
```

Production wiring guarantees the seal happens before any tick fires
(both happen during apparatus boot, with the seal first). The fail-
loud is a tripwire for test-fixtures that boot Reckoner outside the
normal sequence and a guard against any future refactor that breaks
the ordering invariant.

### Test surface â€” replace, don't extend

The CDC test surface (`reckoner-cdc.test.ts`) goes away. Replace with
`reckoner-tick.test.ts` driving `runTick(event?)` directly. The test-
only `ReckonerTestHooks.handleWritsChange` and `runCatchUpScan` entries
are replaced with `runTick`. Existing scheduler-aware tests
(`reckoner-scheduler.test.ts`) and the integration test get fixture
updates to drive tick fires rather than CDC update events.

Documentation gains a new sub-section in
`docs/architecture/apparatus/reckoner.md` describing the tick model,
the `@every 60s` cadence, and the always-emit-row policy.

### Soft-recommend Clockworks

Reckoner declares `recommends: ['clockworks']`. The Reckoner still
boots without Clockworks installed â€” the relay simply never fires.
The standing order is kit-contributed; Clockworks loads it only when
present. This preserves Reckoner-without-Clockworks as a valid
configuration (the registry-only mode) while making the tick the
canonical evaluation path when Clockworks is installed.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-2).

## Verification

- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus test` â€” full reckoner
  test suite passes.
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus test` â€” vision-
  keeper tests pass (they consume the Reckoner; the CDC removal is a
  cascade fix, not an API break).
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus typecheck` â€” no
  errors.
- `grep -rn "CDC handler\|runCatchUpScan" packages/plugins/reckoner/src
  packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src docs/architecture/apparatus/
  docs/architecture/petitioner-registration.md
  docs/architecture/reckonings-book.md
  packages/plugins/reckoner/README.md` returns zero hits.

## References

- **Brief writ**: this writ.
- **Sibling open mandate**: w-moiyh0jz (dependency-aware consideration
  gate) â€” in flight in parallel; that commission's deferred-dependent
  wakeup model relies on this tick existing.
- **Concluded design click**: c-moiw5wkv â€” the design analysis (D-2,
  D-5, D-6, D-7).
- **c-moixb74x** â€” parked future-improvement: operator-configurable tick
  cadence.
- **c-moisx6fx** â€” scheduler kit-contribution registry (this commission
  depends on the registry brief).

yields.output:
All done. Here's what was completed:

**Tests updated (5 files):**
- `reckoner-scheduler.test.ts` â€” `runTick()` drives evaluation throughout; pre-seal section rewritten; defer test now asserts `deferred` row with `deferReason: 'other'`; disabled-source test now asserts `declined` row + `cancelled` writ
- `reckoner-depends-on.test.ts` â€” all `runCatchUpScan()` calls replaced with `runTick()`; Case 5 restructured for two-tick dangling pattern; Case 12 updated for `declined`/`source_banned`/`cancelled` semantics
- `reckoner/src/integration.test.ts` â€” switched to `createReckonerWithHooks`; `runTick()` drives both test cases
- `vision-keeper/src/integration.test.ts` â€” switched to `createReckonerWithHooks`; `runTick()` fixture method added; tests 1, 2, 3 drive the tick before asserting `phase === 'open'`
- `vision-keeper/src/vision-keeper.test.ts` â€” multi-vision test corrected from `'open'` to `'new'` (no tick fired); stale comments removed

**Exports added:**
- `reckoner/src/index.ts` â€” `createReckonerWithHooks` and `ReckonerTestHooks` exported for cross-package use

**Docs updated (4 files):**
- `reckoner.md`, `petitioner-registration.md`, `reckonings-book.md`, `reckoner/README.md` â€” all CDC-handler/catch-up-scan references replaced with the tick-model equivalent; disabled-source behavior corrected from "silent skip" to "decline + cancel"

**Results:** 79/79 reckoner tests âś“ Â· 25/25 vision-keeper tests âś“ Â· 0 CDC/catch-up references in the 4 doc files

  tweak to align with the new shape.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml` â€”
  added `@shardworks/clockworks-apparatus` dependency.

## What remains

The following files still reflect the pre-tick CDC model and need to
be updated. They are independent of one another â€” pick any order.

### Reckoner-internal test cascades

- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-scheduler.test.ts` â€”
  `runScheduler` signature changed; test fixtures need to drive
  `runTick(event?)` instead of the per-writ-update path. Reckonings
  row writes are now batched per tick, not per CDC update.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-depends-on.test.ts` â€”
  same: switch from CDC-update-driven harness to tick-driven harness.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/integration.test.ts` â€” end-to-end
  fixture (Stacks + Clerk + Reckoner). Update to drive tick fires
  rather than CDC observer triggers.

### Vision-keeper cascade (collateral)

- `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src/integration.test.ts` â€”
  vision-keeper's tests assumed Reckoner's CDC handler would
  auto-accept their petitions on creation. With CDC gone they need
  to drive a tick fire to get acceptance.
- `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src/vision-keeper.test.ts` â€”
  same dependency on CDC auto-ack.

### Documentation cascade

The four documentation files still describe the CDC model and the
"absence of a row is a defer signal" guidance, both of which the
new tick model contradicts. Reconcile each end-to-end:

- `docs/architecture/apparatus/reckoner.md` â€” primary spec doc.
  Update Â§"What the Reckoner does NOT do (in v0)" (CDC was the v0
  model) and Â§"Outcome mapping" (defer rows are now emitted).
- `docs/architecture/petitioner-registration.md` â€” Â§"Reckoner
  behavior" names the three outcomes; update the Defer bullet for
  the new dependency-aware defers and the per-tick cadence.
- `docs/architecture/reckonings-book.md` â€” defer-reasons enum
  gains `dependency_pending` and `dependency_failed`; the
  "deferUntil and deferSignal" guidance needs a carve-out for
  dependency-defer rows.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/README.md` â€” high-level apparatus
  prose; update for the tick model.

### Verification at completion

- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus test` must pass.
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus test` must pass.
- `grep -rn "CDC handler\|runCatchUpScan\|considerWrit" docs/` must
  return zero hits in the four docs above (any surviving CDC
  references signal incomplete cascade).

---

# Periodic tick for the Reckoner

## Intent

Switch the Reckoner from CDC-driven per-writ-update evaluation to a
periodic tick. Add a `reckoner.tick` relay handler that, on each fire,
queries all currently-held petitions, runs them as a batch through the
configured scheduler, and applies the resulting decisions. Kit-contribute
a standing order with `schedule: '@every 60s'` targeting the relay.
Remove the existing CDC handler on `clerk/writs`, the `runCatchUpScan`
startup pass, and the per-writ-update entry into `considerWrit`.

## Motivation

Today's Reckoner is reactive: a CDC handler on `clerk/writs` runs the
per-action consider-flow on every writ update, plus a startup catch-up
scan picks up pre-existing held petitions. This works for a single-writ
stub like always-approve, but the design intent (per c-mod99ris and
c-moisx6fx) is for schedulers to weigh the whole candidate set against
priorities and capacity â€” a global view the per-writ path can't offer.
The tick gives the scheduler that global view, and unifies pre-existing
held writs with new ones into a single evaluation path.

For the always-approve v0 default, the visible change is a latency
shift: a held petition now waits up to one tick interval (60s) before
approval, instead of being approved on its CDC update. The trade is
acceptable because the next scheduler â€” priority-walk and beyond â€”
depends on the global-view path being the canonical one.

## Non-negotiable decisions

### Schedule â€” hard-coded `@every 60s` in the kit contribution

The Reckoner's kit contribution declares `schedule: '@every 60s'` as
a fixed value. No `reckoner.tickSchedule` config knob in this
commission. Operators wanting different cadence wait until a real need
surfaces (parked future work).

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-5).

### Tick replaces CDC entirely â€” single evaluation path

The CDC handler on `clerk/writs`, the `runCatchUpScan` startup pass,
and any per-writ-update entry into `considerWrit` are removed. The tick
is the only path that drives scheduler evaluation. New petitions wait
up to 60s before first evaluation; pre-existing held petitions get
evaluated on the first tick after apparatus start.

`considerWrit` either becomes the per-writ branch invoked from the
tick loop or its logic folds into the tick handler directly â€”
implementer's call.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-7).

### Tick handler â€” fixed sequence per fire

On each fire of the `reckoner.tick` relay:

1. Resolve the active scheduler from the registry (cached at start by
   the scheduler-registry commission).
2. Re-read `reckoner.schedulerConfig` from `guild.json`. Validate via
   the resolved scheduler's `validateConfig` if present. Throw â†’ log
   fail-loud, skip this tick.
3. Query candidates: held petitions are writs in their initial-equivalent
   phase carrying `ext.reckoner`. Use the same query shape today's
   `runCatchUpScan` uses (find-by-phase, then filter to those with
   `ext.reckoner`).
4. Compose the `SchedulerInput` from the candidate set + petitioner
   metadata + the running tick id.
5. Call `scheduler.evaluate(input)`. The returned decisions are an
   array of `{ writId, outcome, reason? }`.
6. For each decision, in input order: apply the outcome via the same
   transition path the CDC handler used, write a Reckonings row, and
   continue. Per-decision failure is logged and does not abort the
   tick â€” the next decision still runs.

The tick handler runs synchronously from the relay's `run()` callback;
do not introduce async fan-out. A single tick can take seconds; that
is acceptable.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-3, D-7).

### Defer outcomes always emit a Reckonings row

Every consideration outcome â€” accept, defer, decline â€” emits a row.
The pre-tick model treated `defer` as "absence of a row is the signal."
That model is wrong for tick-driven evaluation: without a defer row,
operators can't see why a petition is sitting and cannot diagnose
deferral cycles.

The defer row carries:
- `outcome: 'deferred'`
- `deferReason` from the scheduler's decision reason (`'priority' |
  'queue_depth' | 'time_hold' | 'patron_policy' | 'other'` for
  scheduler-emitted defers; `'dependency_pending' | 'dependency_failed'`
  for dependency-aware defers in a parallel commission).
- `deferNote: <reason string>` (free-form, from the decision).
- `deferCount`, `firstDeferredAt`, `lastDeferredAt`: running counters
  derived from prior deferred rows for the same `writId`.
- `deferUntil` and `deferSignal`: optional, populated when the
  decision carries them (scheduler-driven defers only â€” the
  dependency-aware defer rows in the parallel commission populate
  neither, per the polling-only stance).

Source: c-modaqnpt, c-mod9a54n.

### Pre-seal tick fail-loud

A tick that fires before the scheduler-registry seal (`activeScheduler`
unresolved) MUST fail loud:

```
[reckoner] tick: activeScheduler not resolved â€” scheduler registry
            not yet sealed. Cannot evaluate this tick.
```

Production wiring guarantees the seal happens before any tick fires
(both happen during apparatus boot, with the seal first). The fail-
loud is a tripwire for test-fixtures that boot Reckoner outside the
normal sequence and a guard against any future refactor that breaks
the ordering invariant.

### Test surface â€” replace, don't extend

The CDC test surface (`reckoner-cdc.test.ts`) goes away. Replace with
`reckoner-tick.test.ts` driving `runTick(event?)` directly. The test-
only `ReckonerTestHooks.handleWritsChange` and `runCatchUpScan` entries
are replaced with `runTick`. Existing scheduler-aware tests
(`reckoner-scheduler.test.ts`) and the integration test get fixture
updates to drive tick fires rather than CDC update events.

Documentation gains a new sub-section in
`docs/architecture/apparatus/reckoner.md` describing the tick model,
the `@every 60s` cadence, and the always-emit-row policy.

### Soft-recommend Clockworks

Reckoner declares `recommends: ['clockworks']`. The Reckoner still
boots without Clockworks installed â€” the relay simply never fires.
The standing order is kit-contributed; Clockworks loads it only when
present. This preserves Reckoner-without-Clockworks as a valid
configuration (the registry-only mode) while making the tick the
canonical evaluation path when Clockworks is installed.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-2).

## Verification

- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus test` â€” full reckoner
  test suite passes.
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus test` â€” vision-
  keeper tests pass (they consume the Reckoner; the CDC removal is a
  cascade fix, not an API break).
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus typecheck` â€” no
  errors.
- `grep -rn "CDC handler\|runCatchUpScan" packages/plugins/reckoner/src
  packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src docs/architecture/apparatus/
  docs/architecture/petitioner-registration.md
  docs/architecture/reckonings-book.md
  packages/plugins/reckoner/README.md` returns zero hits.

## References

- **Brief writ**: this writ.
- **Sibling open mandate**: w-moiyh0jz (dependency-aware consideration
  gate) â€” in flight in parallel; that commission's deferred-dependent
  wakeup model relies on this tick existing.
- **Concluded design click**: c-moiw5wkv â€” the design analysis (D-2,
  D-5, D-6, D-7).
- **c-moixb74x** â€” parked future-improvement: operator-configurable tick
  cadence.
- **c-moisx6fx** â€” scheduler kit-contribution registry (this commission
  depends on the registry brief).

yields.findings:
I have a complete picture. Here are my findings.

---

### Overall: PASS

### Completeness

All files in the "Continuing" commission's explicit scope are addressed:

- `reckoner-scheduler.test.ts` â€” fully converted to tick-driven harness; `buildFixture` wires `hooks.runTick()`; every test that drives consideration now calls `await fix.hooks.runTick()` before asserting outcomes. The pre-seal describe-block is rewritten to the correct "writ stays in new until first tick" model. âś“
- `reckoner-depends-on.test.ts` â€” all 13 consideration tests insert `await fix.hooks.runTick()` after `postHeld()`; `runCatchUpScan` calls replaced with `runTick()`; Case 12 rewritten for the skipâ†’decline behavior change. âś“
- `reckoner/src/integration.test.ts` â€” `createReckonerWithHooks` wired in; `runTick()` called between petition and phase assertion; idempotency test correctly uses two ticks instead of an edit+CDC. âś“
- `vision-keeper/src/integration.test.ts` â€” `createReckonerWithHooks` imported; `runTick` forwarded on fixture; 3 test cases insert the tick call before their active-phase assertions. âś“
- `vision-keeper/src/vision-keeper.test.ts` â€” the one test whose assertion depended on CDC auto-approval is fixed (`open` â†’ `new`). Comment updated. âś“
- All four documentation files â€” updated end-to-end; `docs/` grep for `CDC handler|runCatchUpScan|considerWrit` returns zero hits. âś“
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/index.ts` â€” exports `createReckonerWithHooks` and `ReckonerTestHooks` so vision-keeper can import them from the package. âś“

### Correctness

**Tests:** 79/79 reckoner, 25/25 vision-keeper, both clean.

**Behavioral changes reflected correctly:**

- Scheduler-emitted `defer` now writes a `deferred` row with `deferReason: 'other'` and `deferNote` carrying the decision's reason string. Verified in tick.ts (lines 582â€“583) and the updated test assertion in `reckoner-scheduler.test.ts`.
- `disabledSources` now declines (transitions to `cancelled`, writes `declineReason: 'source_banned'`) rather than silently skipping. Verified in tick.ts (lines 286â€“295) and Case 12 in `reckoner-depends-on.test.ts`.
- Pre-seal tick fail-loud: the scheduler test fixture correctly separates "stamp before seal" from "first tick after seal" into two independent tests matching the spec's pre-seal behavior description.
- The `vision-keeper.test.ts` change from `phase === 'open'` to `phase === 'new'` is correct: no tick has fired in that test, so the writ is still held.

**No regressions detected.** The `stacks.transaction()` test in `reckoner.test.ts` correctly calls `runTick()` at line 1068 after the transaction commits and still passes.

### Quality

**Code style:** Consistent with the surrounding codebase. Comment rewrites are concise and accurate.

**Test coverage:** The tick-model edge cases (multiple ticks, idempotency, pre-seal, defer-row) are all covered. The vision-keeper integration test correctly models the new sequence: petition â†’ tick â†’ assertion.

**Minor quality observations (non-blocking):**

1. Four stale "Reckoner CDC handler" comments remain in `reckoner.test.ts` (lines 941, 959, 999, 1022). These were pre-existing before this commission's diff and describe the old evaluation path. They don't affect behavior but are now technically wrong.
2. `vision-keeper/src/constants.ts:19` still reads "downstream consumers (the Reckoner CDC handler, the keeper's own decline-feedback relay)". Pre-existing; not modified by this diff.
3. Two negation-form mentions ("there is no CDC handler" in `reckoner.ts:41`; "no CDC handler fires synchronously now" in `reckoner-tick.test.ts:381`) hit the broader grep pattern but are semantically correct descriptions of the tick model. These are from the prior session's files.

None of these affect correctness or the commission's explicit verification criteria, which are scoped to `docs/`.

### Required Changes

None. All explicit verification criteria from the "Continuing" commission pass:

- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus test` â†’ 79/79 âś“  
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus test` â†’ 25/25 âś“  
- `grep -rn "CDC handler\|runCatchUpScan\|considerWrit" docs/` â†’ zero hits âś“  
- Both builds clean âś“

writ.title: Continuing: Periodic tick for the Reckoner
writ.body:
# Continuing: Periodic tick for the Reckoner

## Situation

You are continuing implementation work begun in a prior session. The
prior session delivered the Reckoner core source migration to the new
periodic-tick model and committed it as a single checkpoint
(`feat(reckoner): periodic tick replaces the CDC handler â€” partial`).

The reckoner package builds clean at this checkpoint and its primary
test surface (`reckoner-tick.test.ts`, 13 tests) passes. **Cascade
tests in adjacent files and the documentation are not yet updated**
â€” they still reflect the pre-tick CDC model and are red against the
new code.

Your job is to complete the cascades.

## What the prior session committed

- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/types.ts` â€” `ReckoningDeferReason`
  extended with `dependency_pending` and `dependency_failed`.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/tick.ts` â€” **new file**. The
  `runTick()` handler, the `reckoner.tick` relay contribution, and
  the `@every 60s` standing-order contribution. ~700 lines.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner.ts` â€” refactored. CDC
  observer (`handleWritsChange`), per-writ-update path (`considerWrit`
  entry, `runScheduler` per-call shape), and `runCatchUpScan` startup
  pass all removed. The apparatus now declares
  `recommends: ['clockworks']` and exposes `runTick(event?)` as the
  test-hook entry point.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-cdc.test.ts` â€” **deleted**
  (945 lines). The CDC test surface is obsolete.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-tick.test.ts` â€” **new
  file** (742 lines). The replacement test surface for the tick model.
  13 tests, all passing.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner.test.ts` â€” small fixture
  tweak to align with the new shape.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml` â€”
  added `@shardworks/clockworks-apparatus` dependency.

## What remains

The following files still reflect the pre-tick CDC model and need to
be updated. They are independent of one another â€” pick any order.

### Reckoner-internal test cascades

- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-scheduler.test.ts` â€”
  `runScheduler` signature changed; test fixtures need to drive
  `runTick(event?)` instead of the per-writ-update path. Reckonings
  row writes are now batched per tick, not per CDC update.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/reckoner-depends-on.test.ts` â€”
  same: switch from CDC-update-driven harness to tick-driven harness.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/src/integration.test.ts` â€” end-to-end
  fixture (Stacks + Clerk + Reckoner). Update to drive tick fires
  rather than CDC observer triggers.

### Vision-keeper cascade (collateral)

- `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src/integration.test.ts` â€”
  vision-keeper's tests assumed Reckoner's CDC handler would
  auto-accept their petitions on creation. With CDC gone they need
  to drive a tick fire to get acceptance.
- `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/src/vision-keeper.test.ts` â€”
  same dependency on CDC auto-ack.

### Documentation cascade

The four documentation files still describe the CDC model and the
"absence of a row is a defer signal" guidance, both of which the
new tick model contradicts. Reconcile each end-to-end:

- `docs/architecture/apparatus/reckoner.md` â€” primary spec doc.
  Update Â§"What the Reckoner does NOT do (in v0)" (CDC was the v0
  model) and Â§"Outcome mapping" (defer rows are now emitted).
- `docs/architecture/petitioner-registration.md` â€” Â§"Reckoner
  behavior" names the three outcomes; update the Defer bullet for
  the new dependency-aware defers and the per-tick cadence.
- `docs/architecture/reckonings-book.md` â€” defer-reasons enum
  gains `dependency_pending` and `dependency_failed`; the
  "deferUntil and deferSignal" guidance needs a carve-out for
  dependency-defer rows.
- `packages/plugins/reckoner/README.md` â€” high-level apparatus
  prose; update for the tick model.

### Verification at completion

- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/reckoner-apparatus test` must pass.
- `pnpm --filter @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus test` must pass.
- `grep -rn "CDC handler\|runCatchUpScan\|considerWrit" docs/` must
  return zero hits in the four docs above (any surviving CDC
  references signal incomplete cascade).

---

# Periodic tick for the Reckoner

## Intent

Switch the Reckoner from CDC-driven per-writ-update evaluation to a
periodic tick. Add a `reckoner.tick` relay handler that, on each fire,

configured scheduler, and applies the resulting decisions. Kit-contribute

startup pass, and the per-writ-update entry into `considerWrit`.

Today's Reckoner is reactive: a CDC handler on `clerk/writs` runs the

For the always-approve v0 default, the visible change is a latency

## Non-negotiable decisions

### Schedule â€” hard-coded `@every 60s` in the kit contribution

The Reckoner's kit contribution declares `schedule: '@every 60s'` as

commission. Operators wanting different cadence wait until a real need
surfaces (parked future work).

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-5).

### Tick replaces CDC entirely â€” single evaluation path

The CDC handler on `clerk/writs`, the `runCatchUpScan` startup pass,
and any per-writ-update entry into `considerWrit` are removed. The tick

`considerWrit` either becomes the per-writ branch invoked from the

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-7).

### Tick handler â€” fixed sequence per fire

On each fire of the `reckoner.tick` relay:

1. Resolve the active scheduler from the registry (cached at start by
   the scheduler-registry commission).
2. Re-read `reckoner.schedulerConfig` from `guild.json`. Validate via
   the resolved scheduler's `validateConfig` if present. Throw â†’ log
   fail-loud, skip this tick.
3. Query candidates: held petitions are writs in their initial-equivalent
   phase carrying `ext.reckoner`. Use the same query shape today's
   `runCatchUpScan` uses (find-by-phase, then filter to those with
   `ext.reckoner`).
4. Compose the `SchedulerInput` from the candidate set + petitioner
   metadata + the running tick id.
5. Call `scheduler.evaluate(input)`. The returned decisions are an
   array of `{ writId, outcome, reason? }`.
6. For each decision, in input order: apply the outcome via the same
   transition path the CDC handler used, write a Reckonings row, and
   continue. Per-decision failure is logged and does not abort the
   tick â€” the next decision still runs.

The tick handler runs synchronously from the relay's `run()` callback;
do not introduce async fan-out. A single tick can take seconds; that
is acceptable.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-3, D-7).

### Defer outcomes always emit a Reckonings row

Every consideration outcome â€” accept, defer, decline â€” emits a row.
The pre-tick model treated `defer` as "absence of a row is the signal."
That model is wrong for tick-driven evaluation: without a defer row,
operators can't see why a petition is sitting and cannot diagnose
deferral cycles.

The defer row carries:
- `outcome: 'deferred'`
- `deferReason` from the scheduler's decision reason (`'priority' |
  'queue_depth' | 'time_hold' | 'patron_policy' | 'other'` for
  scheduler-emitted defers; `'dependency_pending' | 'dependency_failed'`
  for dependency-aware defers in a parallel commission).
- `deferNote: <reason string>` (free-form, from the decision).
- `deferCount`, `firstDeferredAt`, `lastDeferredAt`: running counters
  derived from prior deferred rows for the same `writId`.
- `deferUntil` and `deferSignal`: optional, populated when the
  decision carries them (scheduler-driven defers only â€” the
  dependency-aware defer rows in the parallel commission populate
  neither, per the polling-only stance).

Source: c-modaqnpt, c-mod9a54n.

### Pre-seal tick fail-loud

A tick that fires before the scheduler-registry seal (`activeScheduler`
unresolved) MUST fail loud:

```
[reckoner] tick: activeScheduler not resolved â€” scheduler registry
            not yet sealed. Cannot evaluate this tick.
```

Production wiring guarantees the seal happens before any tick fires
(both happen during apparatus boot, with the seal first). The fail-
loud is a tripwire for test-fixtures that boot Reckoner outside the
normal sequence and a guard against any future refactor that breaks
the ordering invariant.

### Test surface â€” replace, don't extend

The CDC test surface (`reckoner-cdc.test.ts`) goes away. Replace with
`reckoner-tick.test.ts` driving `runTick(event?)` directly. The test-
only `ReckonerTestHooks.handleWritsChange` and `runCatchUpScan` entries
are replaced with `runTick`. Existing scheduler-aware tests
(`reckoner-scheduler.test.ts`) and the integration test get fixture
updates to drive tick fires rather than CDC update events.

Documentation gains a new sub-section in
`docs/architecture/apparatus/reckoner.md` describing the tick model,
the `@every 60s` cadence, and the always-emit-row policy.

### Soft-recommend Clockworks

Reckoner declares `recommends: ['clockworks']`. The Reckoner still
boots without Clockworks installed â€” the relay simply never fires.
The standing order is kit-contributed; Clockworks loads it only when
present. This preserves Reckoner-without-Clockworks as a valid
configuration (the registry-only mode) while making the tick the
canonical evaluation path when Clockworks is installed.

Source: c-moiw5wkv (D-2).
